{{Short description|Men's association football team representing Armenia}}

| Elo max date          = 27 May 2014
| Elo min               = 126
| Elo min date          = May 1995

| body3                 = 1b215f

| Largest loss          = {{fb|NOR}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|ARM}}<br /> ([[Oslo]], [[Norway]]; 29 March 2022)<ref>{{Cite web |title=Armenia national football team results |author= |work=eu-football.info |date= |access-date=30 March 2026 |url= https://eu-football.info/_matches.php?id=9}}</ref>

}}

The '''Armenia national football team''' ({{langx|hy|Հայաստանի ֆուտբոլի ազգային հավաքական}}) represents [[Armenia]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], and is controlled by the [[Football Federation of Armenia]], the governing body for [[football in Armenia]].

After gaining independence from the [[Soviet Union]], the team played its first international match on 12 October 1992. The national team has participated in the qualification of every major tournament from the [[UEFA Euro 1996]] onwards, though they are yet to qualify for the final stages of either a [[UEFA European Football Championship]] or a [[FIFA World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Armenia|url= https://www.fifa.com/associations/association/arm/ |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20190715120814/https://www.fifa.com/associations/association/ARM/ |url-status= dead |archive-date= 15 July 2019 |publisher=FIFA.com|access-date=16 April 2013}}</ref> The team's main achievements have been third-place finish in the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying Group B|UEFA Euro 2012]] qualifying stage,<ref name="TGMR">{{cite news|last=Ogden|first=Mark|date=11 October 2011|title=Republic of Ireland 2 Armenia 1: match report|publisher=The Telegraph|location=London|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/football/teams/republic-of-ireland/8821001/Republic-of-Ireland-2-Armenia-1-match-report.html|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=14 September 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120914004946/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/football/teams/republic-of-ireland/8821001/Republic-of-Ireland-2-Armenia-1-match-report.html|url-status=live}}</ref> and being promoted to the [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League B]].

The primary training ground is at the [[Yerevan Football Academy|Technical Center-Academy of the Football Federation of Armenia]] in the northern [[Avan District]] of the capital [[Yerevan]], and the team plays their home matches at the [[Vazgen Sargsyan Republican Stadium|Republican Stadium]].

==History==
{{see also|Football in Armenia#History}}

Oldest records of football teams in [[Soviet Armenia]] go back to 1926, when the [[Transcaucasian Socialist Federative Soviet Republic|Transcaucasian]] Championship was organized in Tbilisi, Georgia, with all three [[South Caucasia]]n countries participating.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.eloratings.net/Armenia.htm |title=Armenia matches, ratings and points exchanged |publisher=Eloratings.net |access-date=2014-03-07}}</ref>

Until Armenia became an independent state in 1991, players from the [[Armenian Soviet Socialist Republic]] were playing for the [[Soviet Union national football team]]. After independence, the [[Football Federation of Armenia]] was founded on 18 January 1992 and established relations with [[FIFA]] in 1992 and with [[UEFA]] in 1993.<ref name="AU" /> The history of the Armenia national team began on 14 October 1992, when Armenia played its first match against [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]]. That meeting ended in a goalless draw. Since 1996, the team has been a member of qualifiers European and World Championships.<ref>{{cite web|title=ARMENIA-MOLDOVA 0:0|url=http://armfootball.tripod.com/id29.html|publisher=Armfootball.tripod.com|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=5 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305035316/http://armfootball.tripod.com/id29.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Armenia has competed in every [[UEFA European Championship qualifying]] and [[FIFA World Cup qualification]] since 1994.<ref>{{cite web|title=ARMENIA|url=https://int.soccerway.com/teams/armenia/armenia/141/|publisher=Soccerway|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=26 March 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130326025838/http://int.soccerway.com/teams/armenia/armenia/141/|url-status=live}}</ref>

The first head coach of the Armenian national squad was Soviet Armenian football star [[Eduard Markarov]]. Armenian winner of the [[UEFA Jubilee Awards]] and fellow Soviet Armenian football great [[Khoren Oganesian]] also became a head coach. Many of the early coaches of the national team never stayed for longer than two years. Scottish coach [[Ian Porterfield]] became head coach in 2006 and began to lead the team to some of its first international successes. Under his leadership, the Armenian team played a series of great matches with victories over [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] 2–1, [[Poland national football team|Poland]] 1–0 and a draw with [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]] (1–1), with [[Cristiano Ronaldo]] in the lineup. But then tragedy struck; the 62-year-old Porterfield died of cancer, leaving his work unfinished. An acting assistant coach, [[Vardan Minasyan]], became acting head coach following Porterfield's death. Minasyan learned much from Porterfield and [[Samvel Darbinyan]], another former head coach of Armenia, during this time about coaching and managing.<ref name="NYVM">{{cite web|script-title=hy:Վարդան Մինասյանի ամանորյա անկեղծացումը|url=http://sport.news.am/arm/news/16932/vardan-minasyani-amanorya-ankextsacumy.html|publisher=sport.news.am|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-date=31 January 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130131005413/http://sport.news.am/arm/news/16932/vardan-minasyani-amanorya-ankextsacumy.html|url-status=live}}</ref> On 10 February 2009, after the draw for the qualifying round teams of the [[UEFA Euro 2012]], by order of the President of the [[Football Federation of Armenia]] [[Ruben Hayrapetyan]], Minasyan continued to lead the Armenian national squad, only now as the official head coach.<ref>{{cite web|script-title=ru:Рубен Айрапетян: Вардан Минасян продолжит возглавлять сборную Армении|url=http://armfootball.com/rus/news/2048/|publisher=ArmFootball.com|access-date=16 April 2013|language=ru|archive-date=1 July 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120701152833/http://armfootball.com/rus/news/2048|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|script-title=ru:Рубен Айрапетян: тренером сборной будет Минасян|url=http://armfootball.com/rus/news/2065/|publisher=ArmFootball.com|access-date=16 April 2013|language=ru|archive-date=1 July 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120701152726/http://armfootball.com/rus/news/2065|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|script-title=hy:Վարդան Մինասյանը հավաքականը կգլխավորի մինչև 2012թ.|url=http://aysor.am/am/news/2010/02/11/vardan-minasyan/?sw|publisher=aysor.am|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-date=1 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120601025047/http://aysor.am/am/news/2010/02/11/vardan-minasyan/?sw|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying]] matches, Minasyan led Armenia in [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying Group B|Group B]] against [[Russia national football team|Russia]], [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]], [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Ireland]], [[North Macedonia national football team|Macedonia]] and [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]]. Armenia, considered a heavy underdog, defeated the group favorite Slovakia with two crushing defeats 4–0 and 3–1, defeated Andorra in two matches as well 4–0 and 3–0, drew with Macedonia 2–2 and defeated them in the deciding match 4–1 and tied with the group winners Russia 0–0. The Armenian team scored the most goals out of Group B, with a total of 22. [[Henrikh Mkhitaryan]] of Armenia scored 6 goals, the most goals scored by a single player in Group B.<ref>{{cite web|title=UEFA EURO 2012 Standings|url=http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/season=2012/standings/round=15171/group=700958/index.html|publisher=[[UEFA]]|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=20 April 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130420132812/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/season=2012/standings/round=15171/group=700958/index.html|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Armenia&nbsp;– History and achievements|url=http://www.footballtop.com/countries/armenia|publisher=Footballtop.com|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=9 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130909075646/http://www.footballtop.com/countries/armenia|url-status=live}}</ref> The national team almost made the final draw, but controversially lost in a decisive match against [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Ireland]] 1–2. Armenian goalkeeper [[Roman Berezovsky]] was given a red card by Spanish referee Eduardo Gonzalez in the 26th-minute for supposedly touching the ball outside the goal area. However, replays clearly showed the ball touched his chest and never touched his hands. Replays also showed that Ireland striker [[Simon Cox (footballer, born 1987)|Simon Cox]] had actually touched the ball with his right hand.<ref name="EAP">{{cite news |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/soccer-euro-armenia/soccer-armenia-file-protest-over-keepers-red-card-idUKLDE79C01U20111013 |title=Soccer-Armenia file protest over keeper's red card |work=Reuters |date=2011-10-19 |access-date=2020-12-17 |archive-date=2 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102065643/https://www.reuters.com/article/soccer-euro-armenia/soccer-armenia-file-protest-over-keepers-red-card-idUKLDE79C01U20111013 |url-status=live }}</ref> Despite this, Gonzalez did not penalize Cox, who would later admit he touched the ball with his hand.<ref>{{cite web|title=Ireland 2&nbsp;– 1 Armenia. Cox admits cheating!| date=11 October 2011 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mej41y6G0yM|publisher=YouTube.com|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=2 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102065643/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mej41y6G0yM|url-status=live}}</ref> Had Cox's offence been punished, Armenia would have been awarded a free-kick.<ref name="TGMR">{{cite news|last=Ogden|first=Mark|date=11 October 2011|title=Republic of Ireland 2 Armenia 1: match report|publisher=The Telegraph|location=London|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/football/teams/republic-of-ireland/8821001/Republic-of-Ireland-2-Armenia-1-match-report.html|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=14 September 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120914004946/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/football/teams/republic-of-ireland/8821001/Republic-of-Ireland-2-Armenia-1-match-report.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Edgar Malakyan was swapped for replacement goalkeeper [[Arsen Petrosyan]]. [[Valeri Aleksanyan]] later accidentally scored an own goal past Petrosyan, which ended up deciding the match. Armenia and Ireland would each score another goal. The [[Football Federation of Armenia]] unsuccessfully filed a protest over the match.<ref name="EAP" /><ref>{{cite news|title=Valeri Aleksanyan Own Goal: Armenia Player Makes Embarrassing Play Against Ireland (VIDEO)|url=http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2011/10/12/valeri-aleksanyan-own-goal-video_n_1006329.html|publisher=Huffingtonpost.com|access-date=16 April 2013|first=Michael|last=Klopman|date=12 October 2011|archive-date=31 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131031080852/http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2011/10/12/valeri-aleksanyan-own-goal-video_n_1006329.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Gonzalez had later resigned after the match.<ref>{{cite web|title=Spanish referee ends career|url=https://sport.news.am/eng/news/1531|publisher=sport.news.am|access-date=26 October 2021|archive-date=26 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211026002031/https://sport.news.am/eng/news/1531|url-status=live}}</ref> Despite not getting to play in the [[UEFA Euro 2012]], Minasyan brought the Armenia national team to a record #41 [[FIFA World Rankings|FIFA ranking]], placed the team in a personal best third place in the group stage and went on to become the longest leading head coach of the Armenia football team. Minasyan stated he was proud of the entire team.<ref>{{cite web|script-title=hy:Վարդան Մինասյան. Ես հպարտ եմ մեր ֆուտբոլիստներով, նրանք շատ լավ աշխատանք կատարեցին|url=http://www.panarmenian.net/arm/news/80638/|publisher=PanArmenian.Net|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-date=15 December 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111215132552/http://www.panarmenian.net/arm/news/80638|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|script-title=hy:Վարդան Մինասյան. Մեր խմբի բոլոր թիմերը հավասար էին, սակայն, Հայաստանն ամենագեղեցիկ խաղը ցուցադրեց|url=http://www.panarmenian.net/arm/news/81136/|publisher=PanArmenian.Net|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-date=24 July 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180724213333/http://www.panarmenian.net/arm/news/81136/|url-status=live}}</ref> They were all welcomed in the airport back in Armenia as heroes.<ref>{{cite web|script-title=hy:Հայ ֆուտբոլիստներին օդանավակայանում դիմավորել են որպես հերոսների|url=http://www.panarmenian.net/arm/news/80635/|publisher=PanArmenian.Net|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-date=24 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924063813/http://www.panarmenian.net/arm/news/80635/|url-status=live}}</ref>

After the incredible UEFA Euro 2012 run, the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2014 World Cup]] and [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying|Euro 2016 qualifiers]] were not successful, with the Armenian side stood near bottom in the 2014 World Cup run and even finished last without a single win in Euro 2016 campaign. Armenia salvaged some few pride in [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2018 World Cup qualification]] when the Armenians managed to create a shocking 3–2 home win over [[Montenegro national football team|Montenegro]], which contributed to Montenegro's failure to qualify for the [[2018 FIFA World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://asbarez.com/157051/armenia-beats-montenegro-3-2-in-world-cup-qualifier/ |title=Armenia Beats Montenegro 3–2 in World Cup Qualifier – |publisher=Asbarez.com |date=2016-11-14 |accessdate=2021-11-22 |archive-date=6 November 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191106164551/http://asbarez.com/157051/armenia-beats-montenegro-3-2-in-world-cup-qualifier/ |url-status=live }}</ref> After these disappointments, [[Vardan Minasyan]] returned to lead Armenia in the [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D]], but finished behind [[North Macedonia national football team|Macedonia]], including a humiliating 0–1 home loss to [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/45851081 |title=Gibraltar win competitive match for first time, beating Armenia 1–0 – BBC Sport |publisher=Bbc.co.uk |date=2018-10-13 |accessdate=2021-11-22 |archive-date=10 November 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181110172536/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/45851081 |url-status=live }}</ref> Due to this humiliation, Minasyan was sacked and [[Armen Gyulbudaghyants]] was appointed new coach of Armenian side. The Armenians participated in the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]] along with giants [[Italy national football team|Italy]], [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia and Herzegovina]], former European champions [[Greece national football team|Greece]], [[Finland national football team|Finland]] and [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]]. Armenia lost two opening fixtures to Bosnia 1–2 away<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/match/2019/mar/23/bosnia-herzegovina-v-armenia|title=All results &#124; Football|website=[[TheGuardian.com]] |access-date=8 September 2019|archive-date=18 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190818062629/https://www.theguardian.com//football/match/2019/mar/23/bosnia-herzegovina-v-armenia|url-status=live}}</ref> and Finland 0–2 at home,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/football/armenia-vs-finland/52884|title=World Cup Euro Qual (Sky Sports)|access-date=8 September 2019|archive-date=27 May 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180527010609/http://www.skysports.com/football/armenia-vs-finland/52884|url-status=live}}</ref> and was supposed to get eliminated pretty early. However, Armenia began their resurgence following these losses, with a convincing 3–0 home cruise to Liechtenstein<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/match/2019/jun/08/armenia-v-liechtenstein|title=All results &#124; Football|website=[[TheGuardian.com]] |access-date=8 September 2019|archive-date=11 June 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190611102027/https://www.theguardian.com/football/match/2019/jun/08/armenia-v-liechtenstein|url-status=live}}</ref> before managed to create a shocking 3–2 away win over Greece, former [[UEFA Euro 2004]] champions.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Georgakopoulos|first=George|title=Greece humiliated at the hands of Armenia &#124; eKathimerini.com|url=https://www.ekathimerini.com/sports/241499/greece-humiliated-at-the-hands-of-armenia/|access-date=2021-11-22|website=www.ekathimerini.com|language=English|archive-date=22 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211122195041/https://www.ekathimerini.com/sports/241499/greece-humiliated-at-the-hands-of-armenia/|url-status=live}}</ref> Armenia suffered a minor setback after losing at home to powerhouse Italy 1–3,<ref>https://www.eurosport.com/football/euro-2020/2020/euro-2020-news-armenia-1-3-italy-andrea-belotti-double-as-azzurri-hit-back-to-beat-10-man-minnows_sto7445201/story.shtml {{dead link|date=November 2021}}</ref> before created another shock in their qualification with a 4–2 convincing win over Bosnia and Herzegovina in the same ground.<ref>https://romapress.net/2019/09/08/mkhitaryan-show-romas-new-man-scores-twice-in-armenia-4-2-bosnia/ {{dead link|date=November 2021}}</ref> Armenia was pulled back to the ground when they faced Greece, Finland and Italy in their games, with Armenia defeated in both games and eventually finished fifth in their group, failed to qualify for [[UEFA Euro 2020]].

Armenia participated in the [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C]] and was seen as a minnow in a group containing strong [[North Macedonia national football team|North Macedonia]] and [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]], along with fellow minnow [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]]. Armenia began with a 1–2 loss to North Macedonia away, before bounded back with an encouraging 2–0 home win to Estonia in September 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2029994--north-macedonia-vs-armenia/|title=North Macedonia-Armenia|access-date=18 November 2020|archive-date=23 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201123034139/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2029994--north-macedonia-vs-armenia/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030015--armenia-vs-estonia/|title=Armenia-Estonia|access-date=18 November 2020|archive-date=4 December 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201204045912/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030015--armenia-vs-estonia/|url-status=live}}</ref> In October, Armenia had to play their designated "home game" away from their country in Poland due to the [[Second Nagorno-Karabakh War]], and disappointed with only a 2–2 draw to Georgia and 1–1 draw to Estonia, thus leaving impression that Armenia would flounder from the chance to get promotion.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030023--armenia-vs-georgia/|title=Armenia-Georgia|access-date=18 November 2020|archive-date=23 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201123034200/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030023--armenia-vs-georgia/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030050--estonia-vs-armenia/|title=Estonia-Armenia|access-date=18 November 2020|archive-date=25 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201025151244/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030050--estonia-vs-armenia/|url-status=live}}</ref> Yet, in November the same year, despite having to play away from home, and without their talisman and captain [[Henrikh Mkhitaryan]], Armenia managed what would be the country's greatest comeback in the history, beating Georgia right in [[Tbilisi]] 2–1 before stunned the Fyromians, who had qualified for Euro 2020, 1–0, in their designated home game in Cyprus.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030085--georgia-vs-armenia/|title=Georgia-Armenia|access-date=18 November 2020|archive-date=20 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201120034024/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2030085--georgia-vs-armenia/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.whoscored.com/Matches/1457225/MatchReport/International-UEFA-Nations-League-C-2020-2021-Armenia-North-Macedonia |title=Armenia 1-0 North Macedonia - UEFA Nations League C 2020/2021 - Match Report |website=www.whoscored.com |access-date=2021-11-22 |archive-date=2 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102090008/https://www.whoscored.com/Matches/1457225/MatchReport/International-UEFA-Nations-League-C-2020-2021-Armenia-North-Macedonia |url-status=live }}</ref> These wins had not just ensured Armenia's promotion to [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League B]], but it also meant Armenia could be the first Caucasus country to get a [[FIFA World Cup]] playoff ticket. Moreover, the League B season in 2022–23 could also ensure Armenia a playoff place for the [[UEFA Euro 2024]].

Armenia participated in the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2022 World Cup qualification]] and was seen weak to the group containing strong [[Germany national football team|Germany]], [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]], [[Romania national football team|Romania]] and [[North Macedonia national football team|North Macedonia]]. Armenia started with a difficult 1–0 win to Liechtenstein away, leaving yet again an impression that Armenia would just end up being mopped by the remainders. However, Armenia stunned both Iceland and Romania on their home fixtures 2–0 and 3–2 to top the group for the first time ever, raising the country's hopes of qualifying for an improbable maiden World Cup. Ultimately however, Armenia's campaign ended in bitter disappointment as they failed to win a single one of their remaining seven qualifying games, finding themselves on the end of a 6-0 trashing by Germany and even being held to a 1–1 draw at home by lowly Liechtenstein. Armenia finished a distant fourth place in the group, ahead of Liechtenstein and an underwhelming Iceland side but six points behind playoff-bound North Macedonia and even five points behind third-placed Romania.<ref>{{cite web|url = https://massispost.com/2021/03/armenia-beat-iceland-2-0-in-world-cup-qualifier/|title = Armenia Beat Iceland 2–0 in World Cup Qualifier • MassisPost|date = 28 March 2021|access-date = 2 September 2021|archive-date = 2 September 2021|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20210902153917/https://massispost.com/2021/03/armenia-beat-iceland-2-0-in-world-cup-qualifier/|url-status = live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.eurosport.co.uk/football/world-cup-qualification-uefa/2022/late-show-delights-fans-as-armenia-stun-romania-to-win-third-world-cup-qualifier-in-a-row_sto8200808/story.shtml |title=Late show delights fans as Armenia stun Romania to win third World Cup qualifier in a row |publisher=Eurosport |date= |accessdate=2021-11-22 |archive-date=22 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211122195004/https://www.eurosport.com/geoblocking.shtml |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Stadium==
[[File:Armenia vs Portugal, 13 June 2015, V. Sargsyan Rep. Stad. Yerevan (7).jpg|thumb|250px|Armenia vs Portugal during a [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying]] match at the [[Vazgen Sargsyan Republican Stadium|Republican Stadium]] in Yerevan]]
{{main|Hrazdan Stadium|Vazgen Sargsyan Republican Stadium}}
[[Hrazdan Stadium]] was built from 1969 to 1970 on Athens St., [[Kentron (district)|Kentron]] in a period of 18 months with the financial support of the oil magnate [[Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation]]. A total amount of 5 million rubles was allocated for Hrazdan.<ref name="FD">{{cite web|title=Football diplomacy, Armenia-Turkey: From Ararat's victory to Jorkaeff's goal|url=http://www.armenianow.com/?action=viewArticle&AID=4178&CID=3986&IID=1258&lng=eng|publisher=ArmeniaNow.com|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=6 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306124521/http://armenianow.com/?action=viewarticle&aid=4178&cid=3986&iid=1258&lng=eng|url-status=live}}</ref> The stadium was named after the slope of the [[Hrazdan River]]. It is the largest stadium in Armenia, with more than 70,000 seats. The opening of the stadium took place on 29 November 1970.<ref name="FD" /> The Armenia national team played home matches in Hrazdan until 2000. Several Armenian football clubs also played in Hrazdan. In Soviet times, it was one of the largest stadiums in the Soviet Union (among the top four) and one of the few double-tiered stadiums. Hrazdan was the football ground stadium for [[FC Ararat Yerevan|Ararat Yerevan]]. Hrazdan Stadium hosted its first official football match on 19 May [[1971 Soviet Top League|1971]] when Ararat Yerevan defeated [[FC Kairat|Kairat]] 3–0 in front of a record 78,000 spectators.<ref>{{cite web|title=Soviet top league 1971|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/su71.html|publisher=Rsssf.com|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=1 April 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120401080659/http://www.rsssf.com/tabless/su71.html#71l|url-status=live}}</ref> The stadium would host Ararat Yerevan for the final victory of the club in the [[1973 Soviet Top League]] and in the 1973 and 1975 [[Soviet Cup]]. The [[Soviet Union national football team]] played only two matches at the stadium, both of which date back to 1978. In April of that year, in a friendly game against [[Finland national football team|Finland]], the USSR won 10–2. Six months later, in a qualifying match for the [[UEFA Euro 1980]] against [[Greece national football team|Greece]] the Soviet team won again 2–0. The match with Finland hosted 12,000 spectators and the match with Greece hosted 40,000.<ref name="AU">{{cite web|script-title=hy:Հայաստանի ֆուտբոլի ֆեդերացիա&nbsp;– ՀՖՖ-ի մասին|url=http://www.ffa.am/hy/About-us|publisher=FFA.am|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-date=24 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180624175436/http://www.ffa.am/hy/About-us|url-status=live}}</ref> The capacity of the stadium decreased from 70,000 to an all-seater of 53,849 spectators. By the second half of 2012, Hrazdan was completely renovated to become the regular venue of the national team's home matches.

The [[Vazgen Sargsyan Republican Stadium|Republican Stadium]] was renovated in 1999 and, since 2000, has been the home ground for Armenia. The stadium was built in 1953 and finished within a year's time.<ref>{{cite web|script-title=hy:ՄԱՐԶԱԴԱՇՏ. ՎԱԶԳԵՆ ՍԱՐԳՍՅԱՆԻ ԱՆՎԱՆ ՀԱՆՐԱՊԵՏԱԿԱՆ ՄԱՐԶԱԴԱՇՏԸ|url=http://www.armmonuments.am/pages.php?al=home&id=8855&act1=more&d=2&act=filter&mtype=132|publisher=Armmonuments.am|access-date=16 April 2013|language=hy|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141025121533/http://www.armmonuments.am/pages.php?al=home&id=8855&act1=more&d=2&act=filter&mtype=132|archive-date=25 October 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> Republican Stadium has a capacity of 14,968. During the Soviet period and onward from 1953 to 1999, it was known as Dinamo Stadium. The stadium had its official name changed to "Republican Stadium named after Vazgen Sargsyan" in 1999, after Armenian war hero and former [[Prime Minister of Armenia]] [[Vazgen Sargsyan]], who died that year. Local clubs [[FC Pyunik|Pyunik]] and [[Ulisses FC|Ulisses]] play home matches at the Republican Stadium. In 2008, the stadium went under a large-scale development in order to modernize the playing surface and to create a high level VIP section and other facilities which met [[UEFA]] standards.<ref name="AU" />

Armenia played a match in Hrazdan in 2008 against [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]] after partial renovation earlier that year. The number of seats decreased from 75,000 to 53,849.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hrazdan Holding:Sports Complex|url=http://www.hrazdanholding.com/about.php|publisher=Hrazdanholding.com|access-date=16 April 2013|archive-date=18 August 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150818004343/http://hrazdanholding.com/about.php|url-status=live}}</ref> It is planned to hold Armenia's home matches after a complete renovation in 2012. On 12 October 2012, Armenia played a [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification&nbsp;– UEFA Group B|2014 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match against [[Italy national football team|Italy]], but has not used Hrazdan since. Hrazdan is used mostly for Armenian football clubs and a number of other athletic competitions.<ref name="AU" />

== Team image ==

===Jerseys and colour===
The home gear color were previously the Red-Blue-Orange [[Flag of Armenia|Armenian tricolour]], designed by [[Stepan Malkhasyants]]. All three colors were on the first Armenia national team jerseys issued. The definition of the colors, as stated in government website, is:

:The Red emblematizes the Armenian Highland, the Armenian people's continued struggle for survival, maintenance of the [[Armenian Orthodox Church|Orthodox Christian faith]], Armenia's independence and freedom. The Blue emblematizes the will of the people of Armenia to live beneath peaceful skies. The Orange emblematizes the creative talent and hard-working nature of the people of Armenia.<ref>{{cite web|title=General Information: section the Flag|url=https://www.gov.am/en/official/|access-date=16 April 2013|work=Government of Armenia|archive-date=21 July 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170721211041/http://www.gov.am/en/official|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the Euro 2012 qualification matches, played in 2010 and 2011, Armenia's home colors were red-blue-red, produced by Hummel. Beginning with the May 2012 friendly with Greece, Armenia switched to all-red home colours and an all-white away kit produced by [[Adidas]].<ref name="AU" />

|}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were named in the squad for [[Exhibition game|friendly]] matches against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] and [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]] on June 6 and June 9.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffa.am/en/news/eghishe-melikyan-national-team|title=Yeghishe Melikyan has announced the squad of the Armenia national team for the two upcoming friendly matches in June|publisher=[[Football Federation of Armenia]]|date=22 May 2026}}</ref>

*''Caps and goals are correct {{as of|2026|6|9|lc=y}},<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=9&data=9|title=Most Armenia Caps|website=eu-football.info|access-date=9 June 2025}}</ref> after the match against [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]].''<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=19240|title= Portugal vs Armenia, 16 November 2025|website=eu-football.info|access-date=16 November 2025}}</ref>
{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tigran Barseghyan]] <sup>RET</sup>|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1993|9|22}}|caps=64|goals=9|club=[[ŠK Slovan Bratislava|Slovan Bratislava]]|clubnat=SVK|latest=v. {{fb|IRL}}, 14 October 2025}}

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Norberto Briasco]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1996|2|29}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Barracas Central]]|clubnat=ARG||latest=v. {{fb|POR}}, 6 September 2025 <sup>WD</sup>}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to injury
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby
*<sup>WD</sup>  = Withdrew from squad due to a non-injury issue
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{updated|matched played 9 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Armenia – Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/arme-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=1 June 2004 |archive-date=1 December 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081201133323/http://www.rsssf.com/miscellaneous/arme-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Most Armenia Caps |url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=9&data=9 |website=eu-football.info |access-date= }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Armenia.''

===Most appearances===

! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career

|-

|-

| 9 ||align=left| [[Marcos Pizzelli]] || 67 || 11 || 2008–2019
|-
| 10 ||align=left| [[Tigran Barseghyan]] || 64 || 9 || 2016–2025
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
! style="width:1%;" rowspan=15|
!colspan=7|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Position
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=9|''Part of {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
|colspan=7|''Part of {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=9|''FIFA member from 1992. Not admitted to the tournament.''{{efn|name=omission|FIFA adopted a decision not to allow to participate in the 1994 FIFA World Cup the national teams of those former Soviet republics that did not participate in the qualification draw on 8 December 1991.<ref name=kmcrossing>[http://www.kopanyi-myach.info/index.aspx?page=history11 At the crossing (На переправе)]. Kopanyi myach.</ref> A proposition of Ukraine to arrange a separate tournament for all successors of the Soviet Union and supported by Georgia and Armenia was blocked by Russia.<ref name=hwta>{{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305103050/http://fanat.ua/national/02.html We hacked window to America (Прорубили окно в Америку)]}}. Komanda newspaper (by Fanat)</ref>}}
|colspan=7|''Not admitted to the tournament''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|  colspan=9 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||10||1||5||4||8||17||4/6
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
||10||0||5||5||7||19||6/6
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]

==All-time head-to-head record==
{| cellpadding="3" cellspacing="0" border="1" style="background:#f7f8ff; font-size:95%; border:gray solid 1px; border-collapse:collapse; text-align:center; float:right;"
|+'''Key'''
|  style="background:#cfc; width:20px;"|
|  style="background:#fff; text-align:left;"| '''Positive balance''' (more wins)
|-
|  style="background:#ffc; width:20px;"|
|  style="background:#fff; text-align:left;"| '''Neutral balance''' (equal W/L ratio)
|-
|  style="background:#ffdacc; width:20px;"|
|  style="background:#fff; text-align:left;"| '''Negative balance''' (more losses)
|}
{| class="wikitable sortable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align:center;"
|-
